title: sagina procumbens subsp. muscosa
intro: Sagina procumbens subsp. muscosa, commonly known as Alpine Pearlwort, is a low-growing, perennial plant species in the family Caryophyllaceae. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant has been reported to have health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving respiratory function, and exhibiting antimicrobial properties. Alpine Pearlwort can be prepared for medicinal use through various herbal preparations, including infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, although its use should be guided by a qualified healthcare professional due to limited scientific research on its efficacy and potential interactions.
